primaryStudies.citation: Raun K, Hansen BS, Johansen NL, et al. Ipamorelin, the first selective growth hormone secretagogue. Eur J Endocrinol. 1998;139(5):552-561.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: First GHRP-receptor agonist with selectivity for GH release similar to GHRH; does not release ACTH or cortisol unlike GHRP-6/GHRP-2

primaryStudies.citation: Johansen PB, Nowak J, Skjaerbaek C, et al. Ipamorelin, a new growth-hormone-releasing peptide, induces longitudinal bone growth in rats. Growth Horm IGF Res. 1999;9(2):106-113.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Ipamorelin induces longitudinal bone growth comparable to GH treatment; stimulates tibial epiphyseal width in hypophysectomized rats

primaryStudies.citation: Gobburu JV, Agersø H, Jusko WJ, Ynddal L. Pharmacokinetic-pharmacodynamic modeling of ipamorelin, a growth hormone releasing peptide, in human volunteers. Pharm Res. 1999;16(9):1412-1416.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: SC50 of 214 nmol/L; half-life 2 hours; dose-proportional PK; single GH release episode peaking at 0.67 hours; indirect response model validated

primaryStudies.citation: Hansen BS, Raun K, Nielsen KK, et al. Pharmacological characterisation of a new oral GH secretagogue, NN703. Eur J Endocrinol. 1999;141(2):180-189.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Comparison study; ipamorelin used as reference compound; demonstrates class-specific GH secretagogue effects

primaryStudies.citation: Andersen NB, Malmlöf K, Johansen PB, et al. The growth hormone secretagogues ipamorelin and GH-releasing peptide-6 increase bone mineral content in adult female rats. Eur J Endocrinol. 2001;144(1):91-97.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: 12-week treatment increased whole-body BMC by 11%; ipamorelin comparable to GHRP-6 for bone anabolism

primaryStudies.citation: Svensson J, Lönn L, Jansson JO, et al. Two-month treatment of obese subjects with the oral growth hormone (GH) secretagogue MK-677 increases GH secretion, fat-free mass, and energy expenditure. J Clin Endocrinol Metab. 1998;83(2):362-369.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Comparator GHS study; establishes GHS class effects on body composition relevant to ipamorelin mechanism

primaryStudies.citation: Patchett AA, Nargund RP, Tata JR, et al. Design and biological activities of L-163,191 (MK-0677): a potent, orally active growth hormone secretagogue. Proc Natl Acad Sci USA. 1995;92(15):7001-7005.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Foundational GHS class pharmacology; ipamorelin developed as peptide alternative with improved selectivity profile

primaryStudies.citation: Beck DE, Sweeney WB, McCarter MD; Ipamorelin 201 Study Group. Prospective, randomized, controlled, proof-of-concept study of the Ghrelin mimetic ipamorelin for the management of postoperative ileus in bowel resection patients. Int J Colorectal Dis. 2014;29(12):1527-1534.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Ipamorelin 0.03 mg/kg IV BID well tolerated; median time to solid meal 25.3h vs 32.6h placebo (p=0.15); no significant efficacy

primaryStudies.citation: Sinha DK, Balasubramanian A, Tatem AJ, et al. Beyond the androgen receptor: the role of growth hormone secretagogues in the modern management of body composition in hypogonadal males. Transl Androl Urol. 2020;9(Suppl 2):S149-S159.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of GHS mechanisms; ipamorelin highlighted for selectivity without ACTH/cortisol effects; body composition applications

primaryStudies.citation: Sigalos JT, Pastuszak AW. The Safety and Efficacy of Growth Hormone Secretagogues. Sex Med Rev. 2018;6(1):45-53.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: GHSs well tolerated; may improve lean mass, bone turnover, sleep; concern for glucose effects; ipamorelin discussed as selective option

primaryStudies.citation: Ishida J, Saitoh M, Ebner N, et al. Growth hormone secretagogues: history, mechanism of action, and clinical development. JCSM Rapid Commun. 2020;3(1):25-37.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Comprehensive GHS history and clinical development; ipamorelin development timeline; discusses selectivity advantages

primaryStudies.citation: Bowers CY, Momany FA, Reynolds GA, Hong A. On the in vitro and in vivo activity of a new synthetic hexapeptide that acts on the pituitary to specifically release growth hormone. Endocrinology. 1984;114(5):1537-1545.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Foundational GHRP discovery; established GH secretagogue class from which ipamorelin was developed

primaryStudies.citation: Howard AD, Feighner SD, Cully DF, et al. A receptor in pituitary and hypothalamus that functions in growth hormone release. Science. 1996;273(5277):974-977.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Discovery of GHS-R (ghrelin receptor); established molecular target for ipamorelin binding

primaryStudies.citation: Kojima M, Hosoda H, Date Y, et al. Ghrelin is a growth-hormone-releasing acylated peptide from stomach. Nature. 1999;402(6762):656-660.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Discovery of ghrelin as endogenous GHS-R ligand; explains ipamorelin's mechanism as ghrelin mimetic

primaryStudies.citation: Deghenghi R, Cananzi MM, Torsello A, et al. GH-releasing activity of hexarelin, a new growth hormone releasing peptide, in infant and adult rats. Life Sci. 1994;54(18):1321-1328.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: GHRP comparator research; hexarelin characterization provides context for ipamorelin selectivity advantages

primaryStudies.citation: Kuriyama H, Hotta M, Wakabayashi I, Shibasaki T. A 6-day intracerebroventricular infusion of the growth hormone-releasing peptide KP-102 stimulates food intake in both neonatal and adult rats. Endocrinology. 2000;141(8):2700-2706.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: GHRP appetite effects; demonstrates why ipamorelin's minimal appetite stimulation is advantageous

primaryStudies.citation: Petersenn S, Rasch AC, Penshorn M, et al. Genomic structure and transcriptional regulation of the human growth hormone secretagogue receptor. Endocrinology. 2001;142(6):2649-2659.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: GHS-R gene characterization; provides molecular understanding of ipamorelin's target receptor regulation

primaryStudies.citation: Smith RG, Van der Ploeg LH, Howard AD, et al. Peptidomimetic regulation of growth hormone secretion. Endocr Rev. 1997;18(5):621-645.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of GHS peptidomimetics; theoretical framework for ipamorelin development

europeanSources.citation: Raun K, Hansen BS, Johansen NL, et al. Ipamorelin, the first selective growth hormone secretagogue. Eur J Endocrinol. 1998;139(5):552-561.
europeanSources.note: Foundational study from Novo Nordisk's research facility in Denmark

trials.title: Ipamorelin for Postoperative Ileus
trials.keyResult: 0.03 mg/kg twice daily well tolerated; no significant efficacy vs placebo

trials.title: Dose-Finding Study of Ipamorelin for Post-Surgical GI Recovery
trials.keyResult: Dose-finding study; same population as NCT00672074

evidenceSummary.regulatoryStatus: Not FDA/EMA approved; investigational; development discontinued
evidenceSummary.keyFinding: First selective GH secretagogue without ACTH/cortisol effects; human PK established; Phase 2 failed for POI indication
evidenceSummary.geographicCoverage: Global (Denmark, Sweden, Germany, Italy, Japan, USA, Canada)
